text: شكراً سيد الرئيس سيد الرئيس سيدات وسادة ومثلو الدول العظاء تشكور الجزائر أن العام المرض مدحية الدولية وجميع الدول العظاء على الجهود الكبير المدولة ليعداد وديرستة ديات المختارة على المرحق سالس من إطفقية مرقور والموتعلي القابي إطار خفظ أنبي عثات الغزد في أعمل السفن تولي الجزائر يثم من بارغاً بجميع المبادرات الهاتف إلى حماية البحرية وتقليل التلووث الناجم عن النقل بحرية أنقامت بسنعيدة فقوانين وتشريعات داخلية في هذا الطار ومع ذلك ترى الجزائر وبعد ديراسة متأنيا لمسودة التعديلات أن التعديلات المقطارة بسيغة الحبسيغة الحالية تطرعات الدنياً من الإشكالات الإقتصادية والإشتماية ولذى بين الجزائر تدعم موقف المملكة العربية السعودية والويلايات المتحدة الأمريكية ودول أخرى بخصوص القبول الصاريح للتعديلة تواكد الجزائر أن الجحة يتعدي الدولي ينبغي أن يقوم على مبادر إنصاف والمسؤولية المشتركة ويميظم أن أنتقال النعاد لأن نحو الملاحة من خابدة فيكربون دون الإضرار ومسالح الدول ذات الإقتصادة النشاء شكراً سيدا رئيس شكراً سيدا رئيس لم يأتي لديهم صافة للمملكة عربية السعودية والويلايات المتحدة الأمريكية والمالتعربية المتحدة وكل الوكل الدول التي يأيادت القبول الصاريح التعديلة شكراً شكراً سيدا رئيس شكراً سيدا رئيس شكراً سيدا رئيس ومسأس لديهم صافة لديهم many of the comments in the preceding discussion are based on an assumption a misconception that the explicit acceptance procedure when necessarily mean delays procedural or legal uncertainty and a disruption to the dates and the timelines in the proposed net zero framework there's a simple way to forestall all of those concerns that is for parties that are in favor of the NZF to have the courage of their conviction and send a diplomatic note formally communicating their explicit acceptance to the secretary general on the record before September 2026 fundamentally we dispute Denmark's categorization on precedent and measures call for a different process for entry into force this process is outlined in the convention all countries would need to do is send in their explicit approval as outlined in the convention itself to be clear to the members of the European Union this would not change any timelines the categorization that we are changing the process is such a tactical misreading of the convention itself intending to mislead legal interpretations and dare we say stifle debate on this unprecedented proposal this motion is clearly articulated in the convention itself the logical conclusion of this misreading is that the proposal simply doesn't have enough support on its face for enough states to opt into it furthermore I make this request to all states planning on an abstention to the NZF this motion would provide you yet another means to make your voices heard delegations repeatedly make reference that the default procedure has been in place since 1973 however the real default of the IMO is consensus there is not consensus on the IMO net zero framework proposal to all delegations in this room silence is not consent and you as I have Samuayans, Slovenia requesting the flow of the actual about understanding that some technical difficulties are we will not take these people we will not resolve the issue we will see how we will get that input in with that take seize up please thank you chair and good morning all providing certainty and predictability is crucial to help international shipping invest effectively in reaching net zero greenhouse gas emissions with you the test of the management procedure to be essential for ensuring that the regulations can be updated and kept in pace with technological development this will be of particular importance in the years to come therefore we would like to align ourselves with the statement by Denmark thank you thank you Cesar M. Corp. please thank you chair we have taken the floor to seek clarification on the implications of applying the explicit acceptance procedure the current draft features many dates and requirements linked to these dates including potential payments linked to calendar years we seek clarification on the retroactive application of such requirements to years and dates prior to the potential delayed entry into force sometime after 2028 ship owners cannot manage uncertain requirements linked to retroactive application and there are multiple examples of such problems originating from new legal instruments which incorporates uncertainty on entry into force it is thus imperative to understand the consequences of an explicit acceptance procedure on the current draft legal text and its potential retroactive elements stemming from a later entry into force than the one presently anticipated in the text thank you chair thank you M. Corp. Washington Council please thank you chair during our discussions questions have been tabled as to the support and interest held by industry with respect to the net zero framework the liner industry fully supports adoption of the agreement reached in April global standards are vital to an industry that serves as the principal vehicle of trade among countries in every region of the world which simply maritime shipping is the critical conduit of a healthy global economy uncertainty in what standards may or may not apply to the maritime sector fundamentally undermines the ability of ocean carriers and energy producers to make the investments necessary to meet the standards contained in the net zero framework could simply it is well known that uncertainty is not the friend of any commercial activity that requires long term investment while the current discussion about ratification is certainly appropriate adoption through explicit ratification of the current amendments does invite delay and prolonged uncertainty that might be measured in years or decades it would not only open the door to delay it would also bring into question whether we see entry in the force of the standards at any point in time consequently the outcome is not simply delayed it renders the outcome itself as uncertain to meet the relevant public policy goals outlined in IMO strategy and to establish a workable commercial platform for both carriers and energy producers we encourage the committee to proceed with adoption as proposed in the current legal text thank you thank you warship in council ICS please thank you chair and hello good morning distinguished colleagues we really hesitate to take the floor because we recognise this is a really serious issue for member states I can't emphasize enough the serious consequences of what the committee decides this week he's going to have for the entire structure of the global shipping industry for many decades ahead firstly as I think it goes about saying that we fully support the interventions made by the likes of Denmark, Norway, South Africa, Brazil, Canada to the Lou, Australia and many others as well as our industry colleagues and we support the maintenance of the tacit acceptance procedure as has been explained by many others this procedure has served this organisation well for the last 50 years and all the fantastic achievements of this organisation in dramatically improving the safety and environmental performance of shipping have been enabled by the tacit acceptance procedure and as this language delegates from Liberia, Remarts, it's very rarely that the explicit procedure is used to remind ourselves that this is not actually, this is a very important occasion but the adoption of the NCDF is not particularly unprecedented in terms of regulation I recall fantastic efforts this organisation to make, to deliver the international ship and port security code to expedite the process using this procedure to assist our friends in the United States the same applies to the very radical regulations on the single-hole, double-hole tanker issue which over did the grandfather principle that has been well established I could continue Chair, I just really want to emphasise to amend the states you really have to understand the enormity of the decision you might potentially be taking as my industry colleagues have explained the industry is absolutely desperate for certainty and we need to have certainty as to what the entry and force date is going to be we need to have certainty that work is going to progress next week, meaningfully, on all of the implementation guidelines and at the moment we are in a situation where ship owners, they do not know which ships to order the age profile of the world fleet is increasing so apart from the consequences time I see us we would need to think about the implications of the FRIMO as the industry's global regulator so I hope you contemplate very carefully the decision you intend to make thank you thank you I see us, you're on what please thank you very much Chair and a good day to all your mod would like to align with the concerns expressed by Denmark, Canada, Australia and others as well as several industry organisations we are sincerely concerned regarding the uncertainty that the explicit acceptance procedure would create for the industry we heard several times that certainty is what the industry needs thank you very much thank you, your mod, EDF please thank you Chair for the reasons highlighted by the delegations of Denmark, the UK, South Africa, Brazil, the industry and many others EDF please, it is crucial to follow the testate acceptance procedure in the words of the delegate of Germany we do not have the luxury of time we cannot afford any more delays and the testate accepting procedure is the procedure which provides the highest level of certainty that this will be adopted under the time frame that we have agreed to thank you Chair thank you EDF, Samoa please, via trolling thank you Chair and a very good morning to all distinguished delegates Chair, Samoa aligns with Denmark and other delegations in supporting the testate acceptance procedure we believe it ensures timely implementation for multiple consistency and reduces and means to keep burden especially for some more island developing states thank you Chair thank you Samoa I thank Slovenia, neither the flow but we cannot get them in so are you? Slovenia I will ask Slovenia, Saudi Arabia thank you Chair we would like to raise a point of order we have information that some delegations are trying to access the online portal but are unable to access the online portal currently and trying to make an intervention so we please ask that this is addressed because this this this this course is very important it is important that we are inclusive that all members willing to take part of this conversation can take part of this conversation also we are concerned with the lack of the video being played in some of the interventions we believe that there may be an issue with the system we are happy to provide some space and opportunity for the secretary to address this and we are happy to take it offline and bring to your attention the delegation that is unable to enter the room currently in the virtual portal thank you so the Arabia thanks for your point of order firstly on the issue of video I don't think it is mandatory that the president put on the video they make up on those that are trying to enter the virtual and having challenges they will communicate with the secretary directly so all right they are making contact directly with the secretary

many of the comments in the preceding discussion
are based on an assumption
a misconception that the explicit acceptance procedure
when necessarily mean delays
procedural or legal uncertainty
and a disruption to the dates
and the timelines in the proposed net zero framework
there's a simple way to forestall all of those concerns
that is for parties that are in favor of the NZF
to have the courage of their conviction
and send a diplomatic note formally communicating
their explicit acceptance
to the secretary general on the record
before September 2026
fundamentally we dispute Denmark's categorization
on precedent and measures call for a different process
for entry into force
this process is outlined in the convention
all countries would need to do is send in their explicit approval
as outlined in the convention itself
to be clear to the members of the European Union
this would not change any timelines
the categorization that we are changing the process
is such a tactical misreading of the convention itself
intending to mislead legal interpretations
and dare we say stifle debate on this unprecedented proposal
this motion is clearly articulated in the convention itself
the logical conclusion of this misreading
is that the proposal simply doesn't have enough support
on its face for enough states to opt into it
furthermore I make this request to all states
planning on an abstention to the NZF
this motion would provide you yet another means
to make your voices heard
delegations repeatedly make reference
that the default procedure has been in place since 1973
however the real default of the IMO is consensus
there is not consensus on the IMO net zero framework proposal
to all delegations in this room
silence is not consent
and you as I have Samuayans, Slovenia
requesting the flow of the actual about understanding
that some technical difficulties are
we will not take these people
we will not resolve the issue we will see how
we will get that input in
with that take
seize up please
thank you chair
and good morning all
providing certainty and predictability
is crucial to help international shipping invest effectively
in reaching net zero greenhouse gas emissions
with you the test of the management procedure to be essential
for ensuring that the regulations can be updated
and kept in pace with technological development
this will be of particular importance in the years to come
therefore we would like to align ourselves with the statement by Denmark
thank you
thank you Cesar M. Corp. please
thank you chair
we have taken the floor to seek clarification on the implications
of applying the explicit acceptance procedure
the current draft features many dates and requirements linked to these dates
including potential payments linked to calendar years
we seek clarification on the retroactive application of such requirements
to years and dates prior to the potential delayed entry into force
sometime after 2028
ship owners cannot manage uncertain requirements linked to retroactive application
and there are multiple examples of such problems originating
from new legal instruments which incorporates uncertainty on entry into force
it is thus imperative to understand the consequences of an explicit acceptance procedure
on the current draft legal text
and its potential retroactive elements
stemming from a later entry into force than the one presently anticipated in the text
thank you chair
thank you M. Corp. Washington Council please
thank you chair
during our discussions
questions have been tabled as to the support and interest held by industry
with respect to the net zero framework
the liner industry fully supports adoption of the agreement reached in April
global standards are vital to an industry that serves as the principal vehicle of trade
among countries in every region of the world
which simply
maritime shipping is the critical conduit of a healthy global economy
uncertainty in what standards may or may not apply to the maritime sector
fundamentally undermines the ability of ocean carriers and energy producers
to make the investments necessary to meet the standards contained in the net zero framework
could simply
it is well known that uncertainty is not the friend of any commercial activity that requires long term investment
while the current discussion about ratification
is certainly appropriate
adoption through explicit ratification
of the current amendments
does invite delay and prolonged uncertainty
that might be measured in years or decades
it would not only open the door to delay
it would also bring into question whether we see entry in the force
of the standards at any point in time
consequently the outcome is not simply delayed
it renders the outcome itself as uncertain
to meet the relevant public policy goals outlined in IMO strategy
and to establish a workable commercial platform for both carriers and energy producers
we encourage the committee to proceed with adoption
as proposed in the current legal text
thank you

because we recognise this is a really serious issue for member states

firstly as I think it goes about saying that we fully support the interventions made by the likes of Denmark, Norway, South Africa, Brazil, Canada
to the Lou, Australia and many others as well as our industry colleagues
